source_title: Antonio et al. — ISSN position stand: creatine safety and efficacy
notes: Comprehensive safety review; addresses hair loss, kidneys, hydration.
full_text: The International Society of Sports Nutrition position stand on creatine concludes that creatine monohydrate at 3-5 g/day is the most effective ergogenic supplement for high-intensity exercise. The position stand explicitly addresses hair loss claims and finds no documented evidence that creatine causes hair loss or androgenic alopecia in healthy adults. Renal function remains normal in healthy users at standard doses.

source_title: van der Merwe et al. — Creatine and DHT in rugby players
notes: Origin of the 'creatine causes hair loss' claim; did not measure hair.
full_text: This trial in 20 college rugby players found that creatine loading raised serum DHT relative to placebo. The study did not assess hair loss outcomes. Subsequent attempts to replicate the DHT finding have been inconsistent.

source_title: Lopresti et al. — Ashwagandha and hormonal balance in overweight men
full_text: Eight weeks of ashwagandha extract (Shoden, 240 mg/day) increased serum testosterone approximately 14% versus placebo in mildly stressed, overweight men aged 40-70. Effect size is modest and the population is not healthy young athletes.

source_title: Wankhede et al. — Ashwagandha during resistance training
notes: T rise alongside training, not isolated effect.
full_text: Eight weeks of ashwagandha root extract (300 mg twice daily) combined with resistance training produced a ~15% greater testosterone increase than training plus placebo. Strength gains were also greater. The study cannot isolate ashwagandha's effect from training.

source_title: Wolfe — BCAAs do not stimulate MPS alone
full_text: Mechanistic review concludes that branched-chain amino acids alone cannot maximally stimulate muscle protein synthesis because all nine essential amino acids are required. BCAAs raise MPS signaling transiently but do not produce hypertrophy beyond what adequate whole protein achieves.

source_title: Trexler et al. — ISSN position stand on beta-alanine
full_text: Beta-alanine supplementation at 4-6 g/day for 2-4 weeks elevates muscle carnosine and improves exercise performance, with the largest effects in tasks lasting 60-240 seconds. Effects on maximal strength or short sprints are smaller.

source_title: Guest et al. — ISSN position stand: caffeine and exercise performance
full_text: Caffeine ingestion of 3-6 mg/kg 30-60 minutes before exercise reliably improves muscular endurance, strength, sprinting, jumping, and aerobic performance. Genotype variation produces small individual differences but does not eliminate the ergogenic effect.

source_title: Tambi et al. — Tongkat ali in late-onset hypogonadism
notes: Restored T toward normal in low-T men.
full_text: Daily tongkat ali extract (200 mg) for one month restored serum testosterone to normal range in over 90% of men with late-onset hypogonadism. The trial does not establish effects in healthy young men with normal baseline testosterone.
